creator: Martin.Birgmeier@aon.at
text:
Hmmm... I guess I'm a bad boy here and won't get a cookie. -

What I am doing is mirroring KDE's subversion repo locally. Also, I have 
modified the do-fetch Makefile targets to fetch from that copy instead 
of fetching the actual distfiles. (I am doing this in order to save 
download time.)

For KDE, this works astonishingly well, because they seem to follow a 
very strict release process, such that the tag they assign to a release 
really corresponds to the files in the distfiles they produce. In fact, 
my method has worked for me for very many releases now.

However, in this case there must have been some after-the-fact tweaking, 
such that there is a minor difference between the two. Maybe I can find 
out what they did, although I must say that I did check out (make 
extract) both x11/kdelibs4 and french/kde4-l10n using both my method and 
the standard one, and did not find a difference. Maybe I just failed to 
notice it.

So you may suspend or even close this report; I can always update it 
later should I find out what happened.

Regards,

Martin

On 11/05/10 23:58, makc@FreeBSD.org wrote:
> Synopsis: [patch] french/kde4-l10n (fr-kde-l10n-4.5.3) fails to compile
>
> State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
> State-Changed-By: makc
> State-Changed-When: Fri Nov 5 22:58:29 UTC 2010
> State-Changed-Why:
> I can't reproduce this, the port builds fine for me. Can you provide
> build log? Please, make sure your ports are up-to-date and not
> corrupted.
>
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=151971
>
>
